content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
Re:
Your Request. for Advice
Our File No A-95-367
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1
QUESTION
May you terminate your controlled committee and your status
as a candidate with outstanding debt owing to creditors?
CONCLUSION
Yes.
Under the provisions of the Act,
you may terminate your
status as a candidate and your committee may terminate with
outstanding debt.
1
Government
Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
S of the California Code of Regulations.
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
December 22,
1995
Fred Colby
10221 Avenida Magnifica
San Diego,
CA 92131
Dear Mr.
Colby:
428 3 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Colby,
Fred
Our File No. A-95-367
Page
2
FACTS
You were a losing candidate for public office.
You are no
longer seeking and do not anticipate receiving contributions or
making expenditures.
Before filing the committee’s termination forms,
you
negotiated agreements with your campaign.comrnittee creditors,
whereby all of them agreed to accept a final payment of a lump sum
equal to 50 or more of the balance owed.
In order to make the
agreed upon settlement payments you contributed about $i2,000 of
your own funds.
You have no additional fund raising capability
and do not anticipate other sources of income to further reduce
the $9,360.17 debt.
You have no outstanding surplus campaign
funds and have filed all campaign statements required by the Act
disclosing all reportable transactions.
ANALYS IS
Regulation 18404(b) provides:
-
A candidate may terminate his or her status as a
candidate,
and a treasurer of a committee which
qualifies pursuant to Government Code Section 82013 (a)
may terminate the committee’s status as a committee,
only by filing a Statement of Termination declaring,
under penalty of perjury, that the candidate or
committee:
1)
Has ceased to receive contributions and
make expenditures and does not anticipate
receiving contributions or making expenditures
in the future;
2)
Has eliminated or has declared that it
has no intention or ability to discharge all
of its debts,
loans received and other
obligations;
3)
Has no surplus funds;
and
4)
Has filed all required campaign
statements disclosing all reportable
transactions.
(Emphasis added.)
According to your facts,
you have complied with all provisions of
Regulation 18404,
and thus you may terminate your status as a
candidate and your committee.
The Commission’s jurisdiction is limited to the provisions of
the Political Reform Act.
We have no authority to comment on how
a local ordinance interacts with the Act.
We suggest you contact


Colby,
Fred
Our File No. A-95-367
Page
3
the Office of the San Diego City Attorney for guidance. regarding
the San Diego Municipal code.
If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven. G.
Churchwell
General Couns
By:
wa4jne P.
Imberi
PcVlitical Reform Consultant

CONCLUSION Yes. Under the provisions of the Act, you may terminate your status as a candidate and your committee may terminate with outstanding debt. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION December 22, 1995 Fred Colby 10221 Avenida Magnifica San Diego, CA 92131 Dear Mr. Colby: 428 3 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660 Colby, Fred Our File No. A-95-367 Page 2 FACTS You were a losing candidate for public office. You are no longer seeking and do not anticipate receiving contributions or making expenditures. Before filing the committee’s termination forms, you negotiated agreements with your campaign.comrnittee creditors, whereby all of them agreed to accept a final payment of a lump sum equal to 50 or more of the balance owed. In order to make the agreed upon settlement payments you contributed about $i2,000 of your own funds. You have no additional fund raising capability and do not anticipate other sources of income to further reduce the $9,360.17 debt. You have no outstanding surplus campaign funds and have filed all campaign statements required by the Act disclosing all reportable transactions. ANALYS IS Regulation 18404(b) provides: - A candidate may terminate his or her status as a candidate, and a treasurer of a committee which qualifies pursuant to Government Code Section 82013 (a) may terminate the committee’s status as a committee, only by filing a Statement of Termination declaring, under penalty of perjury, that the candidate or committee: 1) Has ceased to receive contributions and make expenditures and does not anticipate receiving contributions or making expenditures in the future; 2) Has eliminated or has declared that it has no intention or ability to discharge all of its debts, loans received and other obligations; 3) Has no surplus funds; and 4) Has filed all required campaign statements disclosing all reportable transactions.
